concerns.text: The plan validates the braid index (core invariant) by cross‑checking Knot Atlas values against KnotInfo values (FR‑001). Knot Atlas and KnotInfo both derive braid index data from the same underlying Hoste‑Thistlethwaite‑Weeks enumeration, so this validation is not independent and is effectively circular – the ‘ground‑truth’ is definitionally the same source as the predictor.

Cross‑validation of crossing number and braid index as separate predictors ignores their known definitional relationship (braid index ≤ crossing number). The plan proceeds with joint regression and variance‑partitioning claims without a proper strategy to address multicollinearity, which may render coefficient interpretations misleading.

The plan treats p‑values as merely descriptive for a complete census but still reports them alongside effect sizes. This mixed messaging can cause confusion about inferential claims; the methodology should either omit p‑values entirely for census data or clearly separate descriptive reporting from any hypothesis‑testing language.
